behavior_summary: FLOATIN::look: When a player/actor 'looks' at the floating platform, the script marks the item (calls set info with id 0x0208 and process exclude) and makes the item emit a short description by calling Item::bark with the string "floating platform ".

FLOATIN::release: When the platform's release event runs (e.g., actor steps off / release trigger), the script marks the item (set info id 0x020D and process exclude), queries the platform's current surface weight via Item::getSurfaceWeight, and if that weight is zero calls AccumulateDexterity(0x0F) — awarding or accumulating a dexterity bonus.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FLOATIN::look: set info(0x0208) -> process exclude -> create/push string "floating platform " -> Item::bark(actor_pid, string) -> free string.
U8P_FLOATIN::release: set info(0x020D) -> process exclude -> Item::getSurfaceWeight(item) -> if retval == 0 then AccumulateDexterity(0x0F).
Both handlers use the actor/process id passed in (BP+06h) as the target of Item::bark or as the logical actor receiving the dexterity accumulation.
open_questions: What exact gameplay meaning is encoded by the info ids 0x0208 and 0x020D (visual hint, state bit, or quest/UI tag)?
Does AccumulateDexterity(0x0F) apply to the actor PID passed into the event or to some other global/owner context (call site implies actor but the API binding is inferred)?
What precisely triggers the 'release' event in gameplay (actor stepping off, item destroyed, or other interaction)?
Are there additional side effects when process exclude is set (duration, persistence, interaction with other engine systems)?

main_function.name: U8P_FLOATIN::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the in-game short description for the floating platform when examined by an actor and prepare the item state for that interaction (set item info and exclude it from processing briefly).
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_FLOATIN::look) — invoked when an actor/player inspects or 'looks at' the floating platform.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOATIN::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_FLOATIN::look handler that prepares item info and barks the short description string to the examining actor.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOATIN::release
function_map.role: Release/stepping-off handler that checks surface weight and awards a dexterity bonus if the platform is empty.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOATIN::set info
function_map.role: Engine operation invoked to set an info/state id on an item (used with ids 0x0208 and 0x020D).

function_map.name: U8P_FLOATIN::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine operation to exclude the item from further processing (temporary mark used after set info).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/show a short text message from the item to the actor/process (used for item descriptions).

function_map.name: Item::getSurfaceWeight
function_map.role: Return current weight on the item's surface; used to detect whether anyone is standing on the platform.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOATIN::AccumulateDexterity
function_map.role: Add or accumulate a dexterity bonus for an actor (called with 0x0F when platform is empty).

external_calls.purpose: Mark the item with info id 0x0208 prior to producing its description (prepares item state for the U8P_FLOATIN::look interaction).
Exclude the item from processing immediately after setting info (likely a temporary/visual state guard).
Display the short description "floating platform " to the player/actor who looked at the item.
Mark the item with info id 0x020D when the release event begins (prepare/record state).
Exclude the item from processing after the release state change (symmetry with U8P_FLOATIN::look handler).
Query how much weight is currently on the platform to determine if anyone remains standing on it.
Award or accumulate a dexterity bonus (0x0F) when Item::getSurfaceWeight returns zero (platform empty).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: FLOATIN::look: When a player/actor 'looks' at the floating platform, the script marks the item (calls set info with id 0x0208 and process exclude) and makes the item emit a short description by calling Item::bark with the string "floating platform ".
